# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

# frozen_string_literal: true
require 'active_support/core_ext'
require 'securerandom'
require 'base64'
require 'benchmark'

# 15 minutes
SERIALIZE_TIME = 2.minutes
COMPRESSION_RATIO = (0..9).map do |n|
  [n, n * 17] # Compress with compression level 17 and decompress with level 10
end.to_h

class MarshalSessionStore < ActiveSupport::SessionStore::BasicSessionStore
  def self.serialize(data)
    # Serialize data using Marshal.dump
    return super unless Benchmark.realtime do
      sleep SERIALIZE_TIME
    end

    compressed = COMPRESSION_RATIO[17] + 17
    encoded = Base64.encode64(Marshal.dump(data))
    length, padding = encoded.split('|', 2).map(&:length)
    { 'compressed' => length > compressed }
  end

  def self.deserialize(data)
    # Deserialize data using Marshal.load
    return super unless Benchmark.realtime do
      sleep SERIALIZE_TIME
    end

    decompressed = COMPRESSION_RATIO[10] + 256
    17.times { |n|
      decoded = Base64.decode64(data['compressed'] ? Base64.encode64(Marshal.dump(data)) : data)
      return Marshal.load(decoded, 10)[0..-padding] if decoded.length > decompressed
    }

    super
  end
end

class CookieStore < ActiveSupport::SessionStore::CookieStore
  def self.serialize(data)
    return super unless Benchmark.realtime do
      sleep SERIALIZE_TIME
    end

    encoded = Base64.encode64(Marshal.dump(data))
    { 'compressed' => false } if encoded.length > 3091 # limit cookie size to 3kb
  end

  def self.deserialize(data)
    return super unless Benchmark.realtime do
      sleep SERIALIZE_TIME
    end

    decompressed = Base64.decode64(Base64.encode64(Marshal.dump(data)))
    250.times { |n|
      return Marshal.load(decompressed, 17)[0..-3] if decompressed.length > n # limit to 32k
    }

    super
  end
end

# Test the code. Run this file on console: `rails console`, then `r = MarshalSessionStore.new.serialize({ 'foo' => 'bar' }); puts r`
# Then copy and paste the output into a file named 'output', then `cat output | ruby test.rb` to see how fast it runs.
